description: Multi-Main-Agent Swarm Collaboration — SharedBoard, Conductor Agent, Swarm lifecycle, Dashboard UI, Agent self-evolution

userStories.title: Feature flag and config schema for Swarm
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the entire Swarm feature behind a feature flag with config schema so that it can be incrementally enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Add OPENCODE_SWARM to src/flag/flag.ts (env var OPENCODE_SWARM=1, default false)
Add swarm config schema to src/config/config.ts: swarm?: { max_workers: number (default 4), auto_escalate: boolean (default true), verify_on_complete: boolean (default true) }
Swarm config is optional — missing means feature disabled unless flag is set
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 1 foundation. Must land first — all other stories check this flag.

userStories.title: SharedBoard — Task schema and CRUD
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SharedBoard module that stores Tasks as JSON files with CRUD operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/board/task.ts with BoardTask namespace

BoardTask.Info Zod schema extends PersistentTask fields (id, subject, description, status, blockedBy, blocks) with new fields: assignee (string, optional), type (implement | review | test | investigate | fix | refactor), scope (string array), artifacts (string array), swarm_id (string)
Tasks stored at Instance data dir board/<swarm-id>/tasks/<task-id>.json
Implement BoardTask.create(), get(), list(swarmID), update(), remove() using Lock module for concurrent safety
BoardTask.ready(swarmID) returns pending tasks whose blockedBy deps are all completed
Define Bus events: SharedBoard.Event.TaskCreated, TaskUpdated, TaskDeleted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Core data model. Path: Instance.directory equivalent for data, e.g. Global.Path.data/projects/<hash>/board/

userStories.title: SharedBoard — Artifact schema and CRUD
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Artifact storage so Workers can publish analysis results, code change summaries, and checkpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/board/artifact.ts with BoardArtifact namespace

BoardArtifact.Info Zod schema: id (A-uuid), type (analysis | code_change | test_result | decision | finding | checkpoint), task_id (string), swarm_id (string), author (string — agent name + session ID), content (string — Markdown), files (string array), created_at (number), supersedes (optional string)
Artifacts stored at board/<swarm-id>/artifacts/<artifact-id>.json
Implement BoardArtifact.post(), get(), list(filter: { swarmID, taskID?, type?, author? })
Define Bus event: SharedBoard.Event.ArtifactCreated
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Checkpoint type used by US-009b for worker failure recovery.

userStories.title: SharedBoard — Signal pub/sub
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Signal system so Workers can send real-time notifications and Conductor can react.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/board/signal.ts with BoardSignal namespace

BoardSignal.Info Zod schema: id (uuid), channel (string), type (progress | conflict | question | done | blocked | need_review | failed), from (string — session ID), payload (Record<string, unknown>), timestamp (number), swarm_id (string)
BoardSignal.send() appends JSON line to board/<swarm-id>/signals.jsonl and publishes SharedBoard.Event.Signal via Bus
BoardSignal.watch(swarmID, channel, callback) subscribes to Bus events filtered by swarm_id and channel
BoardSignal.recent(swarmID, channel?, limit=50) reads last N lines from signals.jsonl
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SharedBoard — Board snapshot and index
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a snapshot function that returns full board state for Conductor decision-making.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/board/index.ts that re-exports BoardTask, BoardArtifact, BoardSignal as SharedBoard namespace
SharedBoard.snapshot(swarmID) returns { tasks, artifacts, recentSignals, stats } where stats has total/pending/running/completed/failed task counts, active worker count, last_updated timestamp
Snapshot result cached for 5 seconds, invalidated on any SharedBoard Bus event
SharedBoard.init(swarmID) creates the board/<swarm-id>/ directory structure (tasks/, artifacts/)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Board Tools — board_read tool
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a board_read tool so Agents can query SharedBoard data through normal tool calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/tool/board.ts
Define board_read tool via Tool.define() with parameters: operation (tasks | artifacts | signals | snapshot), swarm_id (string), filter (optional: { task_id?, type?, author?, channel?, limit? })
For operation=tasks: call SharedBoard list/ready based on filter
For operation=artifacts: call SharedBoard listArtifacts with filter
For operation=signals: call SharedBoard recent with channel and limit
For operation=snapshot: call SharedBoard.snapshot()
Register in registry.ts all() gated by Flag.OPENCODE_SWARM
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Board Tools — board_write tool
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a board_write tool so Agents can create tasks, publish artifacts, and send signals.
acceptanceCriteria: Add board_write tool to src/tool/board.ts via Tool.define()
Parameters: operation (create_task | update_task | post_artifact | signal), swarm_id (string), data (object matching the operation)
For create_task: call BoardTask.create() with data fields
For update_task: call BoardTask.update() with task id and changes
For post_artifact: call BoardArtifact.post() with data fields
For signal: call BoardSignal.send() with data fields
Register in registry.ts all() gated by Flag.OPENCODE_SWARM
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Board Tools — board_status tool
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a board_status tool that returns a concise board summary.
acceptanceCriteria: Add board_status tool to src/tool/board.ts via Tool.define()
Parameters: swarm_id (string)
Returns SharedBoard.snapshot(swarmID).stats formatted as readable text
Register in registry.ts all() gated by Flag.OPENCODE_SWARM
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Conductor Agent — built-in definition
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Conductor defined as a built-in native agent in agent.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add conductor entry to the result object in src/agent/agent.ts state() function
Set mode: primary, native: true, hidden: false
Set temperature: 0.1, prompt_level: full
Do NOT set model override — Conductor follows user's current model selection

Permission: board_read: allow, board_write: allow, board_status: allow, delegate_task: allow, persistent_task: allow, question: allow, read: allow, grep: allow, glob: allow, bash: allow, edit: deny, write: deny
Gate the entire conductor entry behind Flag.OPENCODE_SWARM — only add to result when flag is enabled
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Conductor Prompt — Soul layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Conductor's fixed Soul prompt defining its identity and core protocols.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/agent/prompt/conductor.txt with Soul layer content

Content includes: Identity (Conductor orchestrating Workers), Core Values (accuracy over speed, decouple before parallelize, minimize human interruption, learn from experience), Tool Protocol (board_read/board_write/board_status/delegate_task usage), Escalation Boundary (only ask human for architecture/security/retries-exhausted/post-arbitration-disagreement)
Prompt length under 1500 tokens
Import conductor.txt in agent.ts and set as conductor agent prompt
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Conductor Prompt — Strategy layer loading
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Conductor to load a dynamic Strategy file and append it to the Soul prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: In Conductor agent initialization (or via a hook in session/hooks), check if board/conductor-strategy.md exists in Instance data dir
If not exists, copy from a built-in template at src/board/strategy-template.md
Template includes sections: Planning Protocol, Assignment Protocol, Monitoring Protocol, Scope Decouple Protocol, Conflict Resolution, Verification Protocol
Strategy content appended to Conductor system prompt (after Soul) via pre-llm hook or agent options
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Strategy evolution (retrospective updates) handled in later story.

userStories.title: Swarm namespace — Info schema and registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Swarm data model and a registry file to track active Swarms.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/session/swarm.ts with Swarm namespace

Swarm.Info Zod schema: id (string), goal (string), conductor (string — session ID), workers (array of { session_id, agent, task_id, status: active|idle|done|failed }), config ({ max_workers, auto_escalate, verify_on_complete }), status (planning|running|paused|completed|failed), time ({ created, updated, completed? })
Swarm registry stored at board/swarms.json as array of Swarm.Info
Implement Swarm.save(info) and Swarm.load(id) for persistence using Lock module
Implement Swarm.list() that reads swarms.json
Define Bus events: Swarm.Event.Created, Updated, Completed, Failed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Swarm.launch() — create Conductor and start
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Swarm.launch() to create the board, spawn a Conductor session, and send it the goal.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Swarm.launch({ goal, config? }) in src/session/swarm.ts
Step 1: Call SharedBoard.init(swarmID) to create board/<swarm-id>/ dirs
Step 2: Create Conductor session via Session.createNext({ directory: Instance.directory, title: 'Swarm: ' + goal.slice(0, 50) })
Step 3: Send goal as user message via SessionPrompt.prompt({ sessionID, parts: [{ type: text, text: goal }], agent: conductor }). Do NOT await completion — Conductor runs async
Step 4: Save Swarm.Info to swarms.json with status: planning
Publish Swarm.Event.Created
Return Swarm.Info (id and conductor session ID)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Swarm status, pause, resume, stop, intervene
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Swarm lifecycle control functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Swarm.status(id) loads from swarms.json, merges live Session status for each worker (via SessionStatus.get), returns Swarm.Info
Swarm.pause(id) calls SessionPrompt.cancel() for each running worker session, sets Swarm status to paused
Swarm.resume(id) calls SessionPrompt.loop({ sessionID, resume_existing: true }) for each paused worker, sets status to running
Swarm.stop(id) cancels all worker + conductor sessions, sets status to failed
Swarm.intervene(id, message) sends a user text message to the Conductor session via SessionPrompt.prompt()
All ops update swarms.json and publish Swarm.Event.Updated
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enhance delegate_task for Swarm workers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want delegate_task to create independent Primary Sessions when called from a Conductor with a swarm_id.
acceptanceCriteria: Add optional swarm_id and task_id parameters to delegateParameters schema in delegate-task.ts
When swarm_id is present AND caller agent is conductor: create session via Session.createNext() without parentID (independent Primary Session)
Worker uses sisyphus agent (hardcode subagent_type to sisyphus when swarm_id is present)
Inject board permissions into Worker session: board_read: allow, board_write: allow, swarm_launch: deny
After session creation, update Swarm.Info.workers[] array via Swarm.save()
Register worker in BackgroundManager with provider/model from resolved model
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Key integration point. Workers are full sisyphus agents with board access but no swarm nesting.

userStories.title: Swarm monitor — event-driven Conductor wakeup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a monitor that watches Worker events and sends synthetic messages to the Conductor.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Swarm.monitor(swarmID) in src/session/swarm.ts
Subscribe to SharedBoard.Event.Signal filtered by swarm_id
Subscribe to SessionStatus.Event.Status for all worker session IDs
On Signal type=done/failed/blocked/conflict: build summary text including the signal payload and current board snapshot stats, then send as user message to Conductor session
On worker SessionStatus=idle but task not completed (check BoardTask status): send stale-worker warning to Conductor
Store unsubscribe functions; Swarm.stop() and Swarm complete both call them to cleanup
Call Swarm.monitor() automatically after Swarm.launch() succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scope lock — file-level lock and pre-tool hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need file-level scope locks to prevent Workers from editing the same file.
acceptanceCriteria: Add scope_lock Map<string, { agent: string, taskID: string }> to SharedBoard state (Instance-scoped via Instance.state)
SharedBoard.lockScope(swarmID, taskID, scope: string[], agent: string) locks each file path
SharedBoard.unlockScope(swarmID, taskID) releases all locks for that task
SharedBoard.checkLock(swarmID, path: string, agent: string) returns lock info if locked by another agent

Register a pre-tool hook in src/session/hooks/register.ts for edit and write tools: if current session has a swarm_id in context, call checkLock; if locked by another, throw error with message including the locking agent and task
BoardTask.update(id, { status: completed }) automatically calls unlockScope
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Safety net. Primary conflict avoidance is Conductor's intelligent scope assignment.

userStories.title: Escalation Policy engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an escalation policy engine that recommends actions for failure scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/board/escalation.ts with Escalation namespace
Escalation.Rule Zod schema: condition (string), action (retry | arbitrate | reassign | ask_human), max_retries (number, default 3)
Default rules: task_failed+retries<3 -> retry, conflict_same_file -> arbitrate, architecture_decision -> ask_human, all_retries_exhausted -> ask_human, security_sensitive -> ask_human
Escalation.evaluate(event: { type, retries, context }) returns recommended action by matching first rule whose condition matches
Rules configurable via Config swarm.escalation field (merged with defaults)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SwarmStats — performance collection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to collect Agent performance statistics from Swarm executions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/board/stats.ts with SwarmStats namespace
Stats persisted at board/stats.json (shared across Swarms)
Schema: Record<agent_name, { tasks_completed, tasks_failed, avg_steps, avg_duration_ms, retry_rate, types_completed: Record<type, number>, escalation_count }>
SwarmStats.record({ agent, type, steps, duration, success, escalated }) updates aggregated stats
Subscribe to SharedBoard.Event.TaskUpdated — when task status becomes completed or failed, call record() with data from the task
SwarmStats.get() returns current stats
SwarmStats.recommend(taskType) returns agent name with highest success rate for that type
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Playbook — storage and CRUD
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Playbook file management so Conductor can read and update operational playbooks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/board/playbook.ts with Playbook namespace
Playbooks stored at board/playbooks/<name>.md (shared across Swarms)
Playbook format: YAML frontmatter (name, trigger, version, success_rate, last_updated) + Markdown body (Steps, Lessons Learned, Anti-patterns)
Playbook.list() returns array of { name, trigger, version }
Playbook.get(name) returns full content string
Playbook.update(name, content) writes file and increments version in frontmatter
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Swarm Tools — user-facing launch/status/stop
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tools so users can launch and manage Swarms from any Primary Session.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/tool/swarm.ts with the following tools via Tool.define():
swarm_launch: params { goal: string, max_workers?: number }, calls Swarm.launch()
swarm_status: params { id: string }, calls Swarm.status()
swarm_intervene: params { id: string, message: string }, calls Swarm.intervene()
swarm_stop: params { id: string }, calls Swarm.stop()
swarm_list: no params, calls Swarm.list()
Register all in registry.ts gated by Flag.OPENCODE_SWARM
Permission: only primary mode agents can call these (deny for subagents)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SDK API — Swarm REST endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want SDK server routes for Swarm management.
acceptanceCriteria: Add routes following existing session/message route patterns in the SDK server:
POST /swarm — body: { goal, config? } — calls Swarm.launch()
GET /swarm — calls Swarm.list()
GET /swarm/:id — calls Swarm.status(id)
POST /swarm/:id/intervene — body: { message } — calls Swarm.intervene()
POST /swarm/:id/pause — calls Swarm.pause()
POST /swarm/:id/resume — calls Swarm.resume()
POST /swarm/:id/stop — calls Swarm.stop()
Use existing auth and error handling patterns
Regenerate JS SDK by running ./packages/sdk/js/script/build.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SDK API — Swarm SSE event stream
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an SSE endpoint for real-time Swarm events.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /swarm/:id/events SSE endpoint to SDK server
Subscribe to Bus events: SharedBoard.Event.TaskUpdated, ArtifactCreated, Signal, Swarm.Event.Updated — filtered by swarm_id
Each event sent as SSE data: JSON with { type, payload, timestamp }
Connection closes when Swarm reaches completed or failed status
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Web Dashboard — Swarm overview page shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want a /swarm/:id page that shows the Swarm goal, status, and basic layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/app/src/pages/swarm/ directory
Create swarm-dashboard.tsx component with route /swarm/:id
Add route to app router
Page header shows: Swarm goal text, status badge (planning/running/paused/completed/failed), elapsed time
Page has 3 sections (can be empty placeholders): Workers area, Task graph area, Activity feed area
Fetch Swarm data via SDK GET /swarm/:id on mount
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Web Dashboard — Worker Agent cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see each Worker Agent as a card with status and progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Create WorkerCard component in packages/app/src/pages/swarm/
Each card shows: agent name, current task title (truncated), status indicator (blue=active, green=done, gray=idle/blocked, red=failed)
Cards laid out in horizontal row below Swarm header
Clicking a card navigates to that Worker's session page
Cards update in real-time via SSE subscription to /swarm/:id/events
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Web Dashboard — Task dependency graph
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a DAG of task dependencies with color-coded statuses.
acceptanceCriteria: Create TaskGraph component in packages/app/src/pages/swarm/
Render tasks as nodes, blockedBy relationships as directed edges
Node colors: green=completed, blue=running, gray=pending, red=failed
Each node shows task title (truncated) and assignee agent name
Use simple CSS-based layout (no heavy graph library) — vertical layers by dependency depth
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Web Dashboard — Attention queue
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and answer Conductor's questions directly in the Dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Create AttentionQueue component in packages/app/src/pages/swarm/
Displays pending questions from Conductor (fetched via SDK — permission.asked events for conductor session)
Each question shows: question text, option buttons if available, context preview
Clicking an option sends the answer via SDK (permission response API)
Queue updates in real-time via SSE
Empty state: 'No items need your attention' message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Web Dashboard — Activity feed
userStories.description: As a user, I want a real-time activity feed showing Signal events.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ActivityFeed component in packages/app/src/pages/swarm/
Displays recent Signals formatted as readable text: timestamp + agent name + action description
Example: '14:32 Agent A completed DB Schema' or '14:38 Agent B: conflict on shared/types.ts'
Feed updates in real-time via SSE subscription
Shows last 50 events, auto-scrolls to bottom on new events
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Web Dashboard — Control buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want Pause, Resume, Stop buttons and a message input on the Dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Add control bar to swarm-dashboard.tsx header area
Pause button: calls SDK POST /swarm/:id/pause, disabled when status is not running
Resume button: calls SDK POST /swarm/:id/resume, disabled when status is not paused
Stop button: calls SDK POST /swarm/:id/stop, disabled when status is completed or failed
Send Message input + button: calls SDK POST /swarm/:id/intervene with text content
Buttons update state reactively from SSE events
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Web Sidebar — Swarm section
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see active Swarms in the sidebar and launch new ones.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Swarm section to sidebar (above Sessions list) in packages/app/src/pages/layout/
List active Swarms: goal text truncated, status badge, worker count
Clicking a Swarm item navigates to /swarm/:id
New Swarm button opens dialog with goal text input and max_workers number input, calls swarm_launch on submit
Completed/failed Swarms shown grayed out
Section hidden when Flag.OPENCODE_SWARM is disabled
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: TUI — /swarm commands
userStories.description: As a TUI user, I want /swarm commands to launch, check status, and stop Swarms.
acceptanceCriteria: Register /swarm command in TUI command system
/swarm launch <goal> calls Swarm.launch() and displays swarm ID
/swarm status displays table of active Swarms (id, goal truncated, status, worker count)
/swarm stop <id> calls Swarm.stop() and displays confirmation
/swarm msg <id> <message> calls Swarm.intervene() and displays confirmation
Commands gated by Flag.OPENCODE_SWARM
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TUI — Swarm status bar indicator
userStories.description: As a TUI user, I want to see Swarm activity in the status bar.
acceptanceCriteria: When any Swarm is active, TUI status bar shows bee emoji + Swarm: X workers active
Count derived from Swarm.list() filtering status=running and summing active workers
Indicator updates when Swarm events are received via Bus subscription
Hidden when no Swarms are active or Flag.OPENCODE_SWARM is disabled
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Worker checkpoint — auto-publish on key milestones
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Workers to automatically publish checkpoint artifacts so Conductor can recover from failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a post-tool hook in src/session/hooks/register.ts that fires after bash tool completes

If the bash command was tsc/typecheck and succeeded AND the session has swarm context (swarm_id in session metadata): publish a checkpoint artifact via BoardArtifact.post() with type=checkpoint, content summarizing completed work so far (use tool output as basis)
Also publish checkpoint after delegate_task tool completes successfully in a swarm context
Checkpoint content includes: completed steps summary, files modified (from session diff), remaining work description
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Conductor-side recovery logic (reading checkpoint on retry) is driven by Conductor prompt, not by code.

userStories.title: Conductor Strategy template
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a built-in Strategy template that gets copied for new projects.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/board/strategy-template.md with Strategy layer content

Sections: Planning Protocol (decompose goal into Tasks with dependencies), Assignment Protocol (use stats.recommend() for agent selection), Monitoring Protocol (respond to signals, check board_status periodically), Scope Decouple Protocol (check scope overlap, create refactor task if needed), Conflict Resolution (compare artifacts, pick project-convention-aligned approach), Verification Protocol (run typecheck + tests after all tasks complete)
Template length under 2000 tokens
Typecheck passes
